Source_Species.reason: The paper studies tomato and describes 35S::prosystemin transgenic tomato plants.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper lists tomato as Solanum lycopersicum in the keywords/materials.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experimental species is Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The 35S::prosys homozygous line was backcrossed five times using tomato cv Castlemart as the recurrent parent, supporting a Castlemart background/backcrossed line.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The genotype is explicitly named 35S::prosystemin/35S::prosys, supporting use of the 35S promoter; the line is described as overexpressing/constitutive in context.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Prosystemin is explicitly the transgene; describing it as the systemin precursor protein is directly consistent with the gene identity.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper refers to a 'JA-overexpression 35S::prosys plant' and reports constitutive PI-II and volatile release in this line.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show higher PI-II and constitutive release of herbivore-induced volatiles including (Z)-3-hexenol and TMTT in 35S::prosys plants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: PI-II is a protein defense compound and (Z)-3-hexenol/TMTT are volatile organic compounds; these classes are consistent with the reported metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PI-II was quantified in leaflets, and volatile emission was measured from tomato plants/leaves; discussion also refers to PI-II in leaflets.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The study explicitly uses PI-II as a direct defence factor and volatile emission as an indirect defence factor affecting parasitoid attraction.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support stronger direct defence, constitutive indirect volatile defence, increased PI-II, constitutive HIPVs, reduced leafminer performance, and lower parasitism on 35S::prosys than WT.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized findings are directly supported by Results and Discussion: constitutive PI-II/volatiles, strongest resistance to leafminers, and reduced parasitism relative to WT.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches were used: transgenic line comparison, PI-II radial immunodiffusion, volatile collection/GC analysis, insect assays, parasitoid olfactometer/cage/parasitism assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper discusses JA signalling generally, but does not directly test or identify specific interacting proteins for prosystemin in this study.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The experiments explicitly include leafminer infestation and exogenous jasmonic acid treatment.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The discussion explicitly states enhanced direct defence may compromise indirect defence efficiency and that genetic engineering should consider both traits.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: A central conclusion is the ecological trade-off: reduced parasitism success on 35S::prosys despite stronger direct and constitutive indirect defence traits.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed uses the 35S::prosys transgene as the manipulated factor and emphasizes ecological outcomes over downstream molecular dissection.

Source_Species.reason: spr2 is a tomato mutant line used in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study species is tomato, Solanum lycopersicum.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Materials and Methods states tomato mutant line spr2 was derived from cv Castlemart.

Applied_Species.reason: The mutant was tested in tomato.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Solanum lycopersicum.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: WT parent was tomato cv Castlemart and spr2 was derived from cv Castlemart.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Discussion states spr2 involves loss of function of an 'ω-3 chloroplast fatty acid desaturase,' supporting chloroplast localization.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The discussion explicitly attributes spr2 to loss of function of an ω-3 chloroplast fatty acid desaturase.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: spr2 is described as a JA biosynthesis mutant and the discussion states it is a loss-of-function mutant.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show altered PI-II accumulation and lower inducible volatiles including (Z)-3-hexenol and TMTT in spr2.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are correctly categorized as a protein defense compound and volatile organic compounds.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PI-II was measured in leaflets and volatiles were collected from tomato plants/leaves.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper explicitly treats PI-II as direct defence and volatiles as indirect defence via parasitoid attraction.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support reduced PI-II, reduced inducible volatile emission, greater susceptibility to leafminers, reduced attractiveness to parasitoids, and lower attractiveness to adult flies than WT.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each of the three summary points is directly supported by the Results and synthesis in the Discussion.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The study used mutant comparison, volatile profiling, PI-II immunodiffusion, insect preference/performance assays, and parasitoid assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper discusses JA biosynthesis/signalling pathway components generally but does not directly test specific interacting proteins of spr2 in this work.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: spr2 was assessed under leafminer infestation and exogenous JA treatment.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The discussion supports the cautionary application value that weakening JA signalling reduces defence and biocontrol attraction, though herbivore attraction may also drop.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper explicitly frames the pattern as an ecological trade-off: reduced defence and parasitism but also reduced attraction of adult herbivores.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The discussion explicitly states spr2 is due to loss of function of an ω-3 chloroplast fatty acid desaturase reducing 18:3 levels below 10% of WT.
